label: Priya Iyer, Senior Counsel at Vitalis Health
why_this_persona: Heavily regulated US healthcare (hospital systems). HIPAA, state pharmacy boards, FDA Class II devices, Stark, Anti-Kickback. Tests the rule set against a US-only persona with a dense and distinctively US regulatory baseline (not EU privacy-shaped).

industry_seed.business_model: Direct services (patient care + physician practice acquisition)

recurring_matters_seed: Stark / AKS structuring on physician compensation

stakeholders_seed.escalation_threshold_label: GC approval for any physician compensation that triggers Stark designated health services; CCO for any HIPAA breach over 500 records

open_notes_seed: We're integrating an AI clinical-decision-support tool over the next 9 months — the AI Act doesn't reach us but FDA and state medical-practice rules absolutely do. Risk-stratification is the live debate.
conversation_style: measured, careful with regulatory terminology, drops dense facts when asked but defaults to short answers

first_turn_questions.regulatory: We're under a Texas Medical Board investigation into a physician practice we acquired three months ago — alleged improper supervision of NP-led visits at a satellite clinic. What's the standard scope of a TMB investigation at this stage, what do we have to produce, and where's our defensible position given the supervision protocol was inherited from the prior owner?
first_turn_questions.privacy: An IT vendor we use for revenue cycle management had a ransomware incident two weeks ago. They've told us no PHI was accessed but we have no independent visibility. What's our HIPAA notification clock, what triggers the 500-record threshold for HHS reporting, and what should I be asking the vendor right now?
